name: Rayuela Display
classification_notes: A highly ornamental family spanning an openface inline (Luz), a dingbat/puzzle variant (Miscelánea), and an ultra-black roman and italic (Chocolate), all firmly in the decorative display category with no body-text intent.
summary: A four-style display family by Alejandro Lo Celso ranging from an elegant openface with Tuscan serif echoes to an ultra-black cursive, awarded at TDC 2006 and ATypI 2001.
description: Rayuela Display is the companion series to Rayuela Text, designed for expressive, relaxed typographic uses. Rayuela Luz is an openface with open stroke endings that give it a half-Tuscan character; Rayuela Miscelánea is a puzzle-inspired font with jumping figures evoking hopscotch and contemporary fleurons in the caps; and Rayuela Chocolate is an ultra-black cut offered in roman and cursive. The family covers Basic Latin with OpenType features including oldstyle figures, standard ligatures, hopscotch figures and fleurons.
foundry_blurb: Pampatype describe Rayuela Display as the companion series for more relaxed uses, comprising three distinct moods: the elegant openface Luz with its half-Tuscan serif quality, the scrabble-inspired Miscelánea with jumping figures and decorative fleurons, and the ultra-black Chocolate in roman and cursive. Rayuela Chocolate received a Certificate of Excellence from the TDC in 2006, and the broader Rayuela project was prized at the ATypI Bukva:Raz contest in Moscow in 2001.
